title: Bidens calva
intro: Bidens calva, also known as Rocky Mountain tickseed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its daisy-like flowers and hairy leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Bidens calva include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Bidens calva is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to take advantage of its health benefits, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Bidens calva is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family Asteraceae. It is native to Africa and is a member of the genus Bidens. The genus Bidens is a group of plants that are commonly known as beggar ticks or tickseed. Bidens calva is classified as a member of the tribe Heliantheae. The specific classification of Bidens calva is Bidens calva, tribe Heliantheae, subtribe Coreopsidinae.

The plant is also believed to have antiseptic and antifungal properties. Further studies are needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Bidens calva.
